Off Calendar Jun-23-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Gordan for (administrator) Timothy Delgman. Matthew Mraule for (objector) Suzanne Mendoza. Timothy Beresky (via court call) for (heirs) Thomas Chase and Steve Knittle. Lisa Desbians, also present.

The Court stated that objections and replies have been filed and asked if there was anything further. Mr. Mraule reported that he had not reviewed the reply (filed yesterday), that Mr. Gordon made a large fee request, that he is now requesting further extraordinary fees for his response, that the estate and the beneficiaries have suffered enough and that he does not understand why Mr. Gordon is trying to allocate fees to the trust. The court questioned why it should consider further fees with respect to issues that were alive at the time of final distribution. Mr. Gordon reported that he has been very ill, was barely able to file responses, that all of the fees are related to the defense and feels that it is unfair to forego compensation for the defense. The court stated that a request for a reserve or a request to re-open would have been appropriate, but not having requested or given notice or alerted everyone that the final distribution was not final is not appropriate. Mr. Gordon stated that he was forced to litigate, that they prevailed and that they had no idea what the amount of fees would be. The court stated that Mr. Gordon could have alerted everyone, could have associated someone in and that the illness is not an excuse for not timely addressing this issue. Mr. Gordon stated that he understood the courts position, cited two cases regarding attorney's fees after final distribution, that he believes there is authority for the court to act in this respect and that there is no prejudice to the parties to fight now as opposed to earlier. The Court stated that the parties carried out everything based on what was before them. Mr. Gordon stated that an extension has been filed regarding that taxes, that the returns have been prepared and nothing is owed, that they do not have the 593 needed for the state return yet, that this form is necessary to get the refund and that his client has a 1/3 interest in the trust. The court stated that the reserve and the refund are to be distributed ASAP. Mr. Gordon stated that he is due $710.00 in costs that were paid to the tax preparer. Mr. Mraule stated that this should have been brought up earlier, that it can be dealt with in Contra Costa County following the appeal and asked why he did not file a motion to amend.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the ability/inablilty of Ms. Chase to perform as Administrator due to health issues. Discussions were held with regard to sale of the property and the need to conduct discovery. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration appointing Eileen Chase as Administrator with limited authority so that the property could be sold with Court confirmation and without bond (subject to receipt of the order). The Spousal Property Petition was continued to October 26, 2011 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Beresky to address the issue of rent and have the opportunity to respond to the percentages, and for discussions on status of Administration. Mr. Beresky will appear by court call. Mr. Knittle's Petition for Letters of Administration was taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Tauscher.
